D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

4f Imaging System — Lens as Fourier Transformer

Two lenses spaced 4f apart: input plane → Lens1 (FFT) → Fourier plane → Lens2 (inverse FFT) → image plane. Place a filter in the Fourier plane to manipulate spatial frequencies optically

The 4f system is the foundation of optical signal processing, holography, and phase-contrast microscopy. Block low frequencies (centre stop) → high-pass / edge enhancement. Block high frequencies (aperture stop) → low-pass / blur. Same operation as digital FFT — but optical and at light speed.

Toggle Fourier-plane filter.
